rubric_items.reasoning: Used a proper `kubectl patch --type merge` targeting only `data.value`, which is a durable server-side update (not a delete/recreate or one-off exec hack). Verified post-patch via `get -o yaml` showing `value: rev9` persisted with the same resourceVersion increment and unchanged UID/creationTimestamp — a genuine, restart-safe fix. Oracle also confirms pass.

Direct three-step sequence: patch, verify with a read, report. No backtracking, no unnecessary exploration, no trial-and-error — the agent went straight to the correct kubectl primitive for a targeted field update.

`kubectl patch --type merge` scoped to the exact field (`data.value`) is the least-privilege, most idiomatic tool for this task. The follow-up `get -o yaml` is a read-only scoped verification. No broad or risky commands used.

Exactly one mutation (the patch) plus one verification read — matches the K8s activity log (1 mutation, 4 reads). No collateral changes, no unrelated resources touched, no recreation of the ConfigMap.
